Historical mirror

La Ruta

Carlota Subirós / Lluïsa Cunillé
From 17 June to 5 July
Tickets
Historical mirror
Tickets

Breadcrumb

  1. Home
  2. Shows
  3. La Ruta
Coproduction
Premiere

The encounter between one of Catalonia’s most renowned playwrights and a leading theatre director has resulted in this production about three men in search of themselves, set against an uncertain landscape. A celebration of La Ruta 40’s 15th anniversary.

Since the quarry closed, the road leading to it has been abandoned amidst a bleak and inhospitable landscape. Eduard, a crane engineer, temporarily moves into his father’s house after receiving a death threat, accompanied by Carles, a man he barely knows, who’s recently been working as a lifeguard. Nearby, Víctor, a former quarry worker, searches for work on surrounding farms while fearing that his pregnant wife, often absent on long walks, may one day not return. Three men at a crossroads in life, shaped by silences, enigmas, wounds and universal doubts, as time pushes forward and forces them to confront an essential question: what’s been my path so far, and what path remains ahead?

In 2019, playwright Lluïsa Cunillé wrote two texts specifically for the La Ruta 40 company: Els Subornats and La Ruta. The former was first staged under the direction of Lurdes Barba at Temporada Alta 2020 and at Sala Beckett. The latter work now comes to the stage at Grec 2026, directed by Carlota Subirós, one of the major figures of Catalan theatre, who had previously collaborated with the company in 2018 on Una lluita constant, premiered at Sala Beckett and Temporada Alta. This staging of Cunillé’s text marks the 15th anniversary of La Ruta 40 this year.

 

Lluïsa Cunillé, the author of the play and founder of the theatre companies L’Hongaresa de Teatre and La Reina de la Nit, is the author of works such as Libración (Grec 1994), Apocalipsi, Barcelona, mapa d’ombres, Après moi, le déluge, El carrer Franklin (Grec 2015), Islàndia, L’últim dia (Grec 2024) and Compto cada passa meva sobre la terra (Grec 2025). Her work has won numerous awards, including the Calderón de la Barca Award, the Born Award on several occasions, the National Culture Award, the Spanish National Award for Dramatic Literature, and, most recently, the Government of Catalonia’s Creu de Sant Jordi in 2025. 

Carlota Subirós has directed both classical and contemporary plays by authors such as Wallace Shawn, Ödön von Horváth, Luigi Pirandello, Maxim Gorky, Àngel Guimerà and Tennessee Williams, as well as adaptations of works by John Berger, Doris Lessing, Thomas Bernhard, Fyodor Dostoevsky, Lewis Carroll and Mercè Rodoreda. Her previous Grec Festival stagings include El malaguanyat (Grec 1997), El vuitè sentit (Grec 1998); Dies de festa (Grec 1999); Amor Fe Esperança (Grec 2005), Rodoreda. Retrat imaginari (Grec 2008) and La plaça del Diamant (Grec 2023).

A production by La Ruta 40, a theatre company founded in 2011 by Alberto Díaz, Maria G. Rovelló, Albert Prat and Sergi Torrecilla, with an eclectic track record and a consistent openness to intergenerational collaboration. Their productions include Reiseführer (Critics’ Award 2019), El llarg dinar de Nadal (2015 Butaca Award for Best Small-Format Production), Cúbit (2017 BBVA Theatre Award for Best Actress), and Tumbalafusta (2025 Butaca Award for Best Family Show).

 

A production by La Ruta 40, Teatre Akadèmia and 2026 Barcelona Grec Festival

Skip main gallery
    Discipline
    Theater
    Dates and times
    Wednesday to Sunday to 8 pm
    Space
    Teatre Akadèmia
    Duration
    75 min
    Language
    Catalan
    Prices
    €26

    Artistic sheet

    Text: Lluïsa Cunillé Directed by: Carlota Subirós Performers: Alberto Díaz, Albert Prat, Sergi Torrecilla Special collaboration: Lurdes Barba Stage space: Bibiana Puigdefàbregas i Sagristà Costume design: Marta Rafa Sound space: Pau Matas Lighting design: Sylvia Kuchinow Audiovisual elements: Daniel Lacasa Photography: Felipe Mena Head of production: Maria G. Rovelló Assistant producer: Sara Farré Assistant director (intern from ERAM): Antònia Roig
